reasoning_chain_text: 1. In the 2013-2014 high-school performance data, identify NYC high school DBNs with both an A progress report grade and an A performance grade in 2010-2011, then keep the ones that also had an A progress report grade in 2011-2012.
2. In the later high-school performance data, from the remaining DBNs keep the school with college/career readiness between 98% and 99% in both 2012 and 2013 and a 2013 graduation rate below 100%.
3. Determine the high school name for the remaining DBN.
4. Determine the person after whom that high school is named.
5. Determine the university where that person studied.
6. Using the university identified in step 5, determine the year it was founded.
original_final_question: Find the NYC high school that got an A on its progress report in 2010–11 and 2011–12, was also rated A for performance in 2010–11, and later had college/career readiness between 98-99% in both 2012 and 2013, but with a 2013 graduation rate below 100%. Fnd the person after whom is that school named. Locate the university he study, and tell me in what year was that university founded?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

Node 3: 2012 and 2013 CCR between 98-99% (not 100%) and 2013 graduation rate below 100% -> {02M475}
Result -> <hop 2 answer> = {02M475}
HOP 3 (d=1, data.gov 2011-2012 high school progress report - node 4 depends on <hop 2 answer>):

HOP 4 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 5 depends on <hop 3 answer>):

HOP 5 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 6 depends on <hop 4 answer>):

HOP 6 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 7 depends on <hop 5 answer>):

Result -> <hop 6 answer> = 1585
Final answer: 1585
